task: Add a small export adapter without changing the existing default path.
headline: Keep the current default stable while adding one verified adapter path.
approach_summary: The plan separates source-format changes from adapter output, adds verifier coverage before broad rewrites, and leaves old defaults untouched until samples are reviewed.

steps.title: Pin the input and output contract
steps.summary: Write down the exact source fields and adapter fields before editing code.
steps.what: Inspect the current exporter, examples, and consumer expectations. Record the source contract separately from the adapter contract so later steps do not mix them.
steps.verification: A reviewer can identify the source format, adapter format, and compatibility boundary from one doc section.

steps.title: Build a verifier over mock examples
steps.summary: Create small examples that fail on missing fields, wrong masks, and bad coordinates.
steps.what: Add mock rows and a verifier that checks structure before any training or downstream consumption starts.
steps.verification: The verifier catches at least one intentionally malformed mock for each high-risk contract rule.

steps.title: Implement the adapter as a narrow path
steps.summary: Reuse existing serialization helpers and keep the default export unchanged.
steps.what: Add the adapter behind an explicit flag or command while reusing existing parsing and validation helpers.
steps.verification: A smoke run produces expected JSONL and the previous default command still produces byte-for-byte comparable output on the mock set.

deferred: Full dataset run after the browser-reviewed sample passes.
Removing legacy output only after the new consumer confirms compatibility.
open_questions: Should the adapter become the default in the next release or remain opt-in?

alternatives_considered.approach: Replace the exporter in one step
alternatives_considered.why_not: It increases blast radius before verifier coverage exists.
